gaps.requirement: Shape 1a — api compose-VALIDATION + outcome-report: reserve-then-spend delivery-token consumption, composedTextRef hash-equality binding, and the ONE authoritative delivery-outcome audit entry (AD-EXT-3 token-lifecycle, AD-EXT-6 audit-SoR, §8 Shape 1a).
gaps.quote: api's Shape 1a compose-validation call RESERVES/validates the token; it does NOT mark it spent and does NOT write the audit. The client then runs the AD-EXT-4 canary ... and reports the TERMINAL outcome — injected | copied | aborted — back to api over Shape 1a's outcome-report; api marks the token spent and writes the ONE authoritative audit entry reflecting the REAL mechanism only on that outcome-report ... api validates presented.composedTextRef == token.composedTextRef by HASH EQUALITY ONLY — api never re-derives the text.
gaps.why_uncovered: Server counterparty of the extension CLIENT stories ENG-2711..2730, which only PRESENT the token and REPORT the outcome. No api story implements the validate/reserve/spend/audit side: ENG-2311 is enrichment-time private-memory egress audit (FR-SA10, api's own weave), ENG-2313 is the RYW SoR read, ENG-2307 is capture-time privacy. The two-phase reserve-then-spend consumption ledger + hash-equality composedTextRef check + single authoritative delivery audit is a NEW, unowned delivery seam.

proposed_issue.title: [api] Shape 1a compose-validation + outcome-report — reserve-then-spend delivery-token consumption, composedTextRef hash-equality binding & the one authoritative delivery audit
proposed_issue.milestone: NEW: B10 — Cross-AI delivery seam (extension server counterparties)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As **`orvex-studio-api`** (the delivery-seam server counterparty of the untrusted extension client), I want a **Shape 1a compose-VALIDATION + outcome-report pair** that RESERVES a presented identity-minted delivery token by HASH-EQUALITY on `composedTextRef`, and on a SEPARATE terminal outcome-report SPENDS the token and writes the ONE authoritative audit entry, so that an untrusted client can never swap composed content after minting, never double-spend a single-use token, and the server audit (not the client log) is the sole compliance system-of-record.

**Definition of Done:** `TestComposeValidateReserveThenSpendAudit` (integration) — a presented `{providerId, target, deliveryToken, composedTextRef}` returns `{validated}` and RESERVES (does not spend, writes no audit) only when `presented.composedTextRef == token.composedTextRef` by hash equality; a mismatched ref returns `{refused}`; a following `{deliveryToken, outcome: injected|copied|aborted}` outcome-report returns `{acknowledged}`, marks the token spent, and writes exactly ONE audit entry reflecting the real mechanism + delivered `composedTextRef`; a second outcome-report on the same token is rejected (single-use).
*Dispatch-blocked until the Shape 1a contract TAG exists (ENG-2690 AC5, Wave-1-gated on ENG-2037/ENG-2091) and the api pack certifies (ENG-2098).*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a presented `{providerId, target, deliveryToken, composedTextRef}`, When `presented.composedTextRef == token.composedTextRef` by HASH EQUALITY ONLY (api never re-derives or re-serves the text), Then return `{validated}` and RESERVE the token — do NOT mark it spent, do NOT write the audit. *assert: reserved-not-spent, no audit row yet* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §8 Shape 1a, §2 AD-EXT-3]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given a `composedTextRef` that does not hash-match the token, When validation runs, Then return `{refused}` and reserve nothing (closes the post-mint content-swap gap). *assert: mismatch → refused, no reserve* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 AD-EXT-3, §8 Shape 2 Note]
- [ ] **AC3** — Given a reserved token, When the client posts `{deliveryToken, outcome: injected|copied|aborted}` to the outcome-report leg, Then return `{acknowledged}`, mark the token SPENT, and write exactly ONE authoritative audit entry reflecting the REAL mechanism and the delivered `composedTextRef`. *assert: exactly-one audit row, mechanism == reported outcome* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 AD-EXT-3 token-lifecycle, §2 AD-EXT-6 audit-SoR]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given a GO-provider canary that failed and fell through to copy, When it reports `outcome: copied` under the SAME reserved token, Then it is accepted as the same per-use action — no second mint, no second consent. *assert: canary-fail→copy spends the same token once* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §4 DEGRADE-variant, §2 AD-EXT-4 tie-in]
- [ ] **AC5** (negative) — Given an already-spent token, When a second outcome-report or a re-validate arrives, Then it is rejected (single-use enforced at the reserve/spend ledger). *assert: replay → rejected* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 AD-EXT-3, §8 Shape 2 token]
- [ ] **AC6** — Given the outcome-report leg, When it responds, Then it returns an `{acknowledged}` receipt and NEVER an AD-5a `{ok|needs-manual-paste}` DOM outcome (ordering invariant; api decides validity only). *assert: api never emits the Shape 1b DOM outcome* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 AD-EXT-3 ordering-invariant, §8 Shape 1a Note]
- [ ] **AC7** — Given AD-EXT-7 read-your-writes, When the audit + delivery are recorded, Then the confirming user's just-confirmed card is served from the api SoR until the knowledge projection catches up (audit entry is RYW-relevant). *assert: RYW holds for the just-confirmed card* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 AD-EXT-7, §8 Shape 1a Note]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: tests for hash-equality validate (AC1/2), reserve-then-spend ledger + single-use replay (AC3/5), canary-fail→copy same-token spend (AC4), no-DOM-outcome leak (AC6), RYW-on-audit (AC7).
- [ ] GREEN: `POST` compose-validation endpoint — introspect + hash-equality-check the identity-minted delivery token (via `pkg/identityclient` introspection), RESERVE in a delivery-token consumption ledger (Postgres), never re-derive the text.
- [ ] GREEN: `POST` outcome-report leg — SPEND the reserved token + write the ONE authoritative delivery audit entry (mechanism + delivered `composedTextRef`), out-of-band per NFR-6 SoR.
- [ ] Store: delivery-token consumption ledger (reserved/spent state, single-use, short-TTL expiry, `composedTextRef`, scope) + authoritative delivery-audit table, both cell-stamped.
- [ ] Codegen the Shape 1a client/server types from `orvex-studio-contracts` — no hand-rolled types, no `any` across the seam (CS ❌#12).

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the extension canon sweep (`ViWUZj1MrW`, Architecture: Orvex Studio Extension — Cross-AI Delivery — status DRAFT, pending human doc-ratify per CS §12; reviewer-gate PASSed (opus-adversarial-review), all six extension-pack pages drafted and awaiting PO doc-ratify, none yet canonical).

Server counterparty of the extension CLIENT stories (ENG-2711..2730) — those PRESENT the token and REPORT the outcome; NO api story implements the validate/reserve/spend/audit side. Verified NOT owned: ENG-2311 (`[api]` Private-Memory guard + per-use consent + egress audit) is api's OWN enrichment-time weave audit (FR-SA10), NOT the extension delivery-token validation; ENG-2313 (`[api]` Composer RYW) is the SoR read only; ENG-2307 (`[api]` 3-state privacy/consent pipeline) is capture-time. The reserve-then-spend two-phase consumption + the ONE authoritative delivery audit is a NEW delivery seam.
**Must-resolve at pack review (ENG-2098):** `composedTextRef` is `composeId/content-hash` — pin whether the composeId is server-ISSUED (api/`ai` at compose, ENG-2291) or client-computed content-hash; either way api validates by hash equality and never re-derives (§8 Shape 1a Note). Cross-service: the mint side is identity (ENG-2448 + its Shape-2 tightening); the token flows identity(mint)→api(reserve/spend).

## 🧪 Testing

`TestComposeValidateReserveThenSpendAudit` (integration). CS §5 mock-only-true-boundaries: exercise the reserve/spend ledger + audit write REAL; the identity token mint and the client canary are cross-service boundaries stubbed at their edges. Unit: hash-equality validator, single-use replay guard.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§0/3.7/5/9/10/11/12 (contract-shape law, design-it-twice, mock-only-true-boundaries, honesty). SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: Security (Zero-Trust, mint-boundary enforcement, personal↔employer firewall), Reliability (fail-loud/fail-safe), Operational Excellence (single authoritative audit). cell-lint (audit events cell-stamped; `internal/store/postgres/` confinement).

## 🔗 References

Architecture `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 (AD-EXT-3/4/6/7/8), §4 sequence, §8 Shape 1a/1b/2. api PRD `85qj2wwU2L`; api Architecture `ekTh7nDQqo`.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2690** (extension definition pack — Shape 1a contract TAG, AC5 dispatch gate), Wave-1-gated on **ENG-2037** / **ENG-2091** (contracts seam).
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2098** (api Composer pack certification / contract tag).
- [ ] Cross-service: **ENG-2448** (identity delivery-token mint — supplies the token api reserves/spends). Consumed by extension clients **ENG-2717** (adapter framework), **ENG-2721** (consent gate), **ENG-2722** (firewall) which PRESENT the token.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.verdict_note: CONFIRMED — verified against live wiki source (ViWUZj1MrW §8 Shape 1a, §2 AD-EXT-3/4/6/7, §14 Finding 1/2/4/5) word-for-word; grepped breakdown/*/plan.json + bodies for composedTextRef/Shape 1a/reserve-spend — zero hits; confirmed ENG-2307/2311/2313 (api E3-S3/E4-S2/E4-S4) cover privacy/enrichment-consent/RYW only, not delivery-token validate/reserve/spend. Fixed: body_md overclaimed ViWUZj1MrW as ratified/CANONICAL — page is status=DRAFT pending human doc-ratify (CS §12); reworded in place, verdict unaffected since this program routinely files dispatch-blocked tickets against reviewer-gate-passed draft architecture (established precedent, e.g. NFR budgets/ADRs filed operative-pending-ratify).

gaps.requirement: Shape 3 — orvex-studio-api canary-telemetry ingest endpoint: accept the classified breakage event, re-emit as an OTLP metric canary_trip_total + a Loki log line (never a CloudEvent), and drive the FR-D7 paging alert per (provider + selectorPackVersion + failureClass) to the extension on-call (§3 canary-telemetry routing, §6, §8 Shape 3).
gaps.quote: The service worker POSTs the classified event (Shape 3: providerId, selectorPackVersion, failureClass, tier, timestamp) to orvex-studio-api's canary-telemetry ingest endpoint over the SAME generated TS client used for the compose call — api is the sole ingesting Orvex service; there is no separate telemetry service to invent. api re-emits the event as an OTLP metric (canary_trip_total{provider,class,selectorPackVersion}) and a structured Loki log line that drives the paging alert; it is never re-emitted as a CloudEvent.
gaps.why_uncovered: Server counterparty of extension canary clients ENG-2725/ENG-2726, which DETECT and POST. No api story implements the ingest endpoint, the OTLP/Loki re-emit, or the FR-D7 alert rule: api's own E1-S6 observability/audit stream emits api's OWN OTel SLIs, and the console alert-state surface (L1NVnmRgpy) only renders existing alerts — it does not define the canary rule. The architecture explicitly names api as the SOLE ingesting service; the ingest + emit + alert-rule is unowned.

proposed_issue.title: [api] Shape 3 canary-telemetry ingest — accept extension breakage events, re-emit OTLP canary_trip_total metric + Loki log (never CloudEvent) & drive the FR-D7 per-(provider,version,class) paging alert
proposed_issue.milestone: NEW: B10 — Cross-AI delivery seam (extension server counterparties)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As **`orvex-studio-api`** (the SOLE Orvex service the isolated-world extension can reach — no broker, no collector/agent reachable under AD-EXT-5), I want a **Shape 3 canary-telemetry ingest endpoint** that accepts a classified breakage event over the same generated TS client, re-emits it as an OTLP metric `canary_trip_total{provider,class,selectorPackVersion}` plus a structured Loki log line, and drives a paging alert per (provider + selector-version + class), so that a provider-DOM breakage pages the extension on-call BEFORE broad user impact (FR-D7) and never rides the `studio-spine` CloudEvent bus.

**Definition of Done:** `TestCanaryTelemetryIngestEmitsMetricLogAndAlerts` (integration) — a posted `{providerId, selectorPackVersion, failureClass in {NOTFOUND,NOTWRITABLE,AMBIGUOUS,VERIFY}, tier, timestamp}` is ingested, re-emitted as an OTLP `canary_trip_total` metric AND a Loki log line, and NEVER re-emitted as a CloudEvent; a firing alert routes to the extension on-call, with a DISTINCT alert per (provider + selectorPackVersion + class).
*Dispatch-blocked until the Shape 3 contract TAG exists (ENG-2690 AC5, Wave-1-gated on ENG-2037/ENG-2091) and the api pack certifies (ENG-2098).*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a POST of `{providerId, selectorPackVersion, failureClass, tier, timestamp}`, When ingested, Then it validates against the Shape 3 generated type (no `any`) and is accepted over the same TS client used for the compose call. *assert: schema-valid event accepted, malformed rejected* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §8 Shape 3, §3 canary-telemetry routing]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given an accepted event, When re-emitted, Then it becomes an OTLP metric `canary_trip_total{provider,class,selectorPackVersion}` AND a structured Loki log line. *assert: metric + log line both emitted with the labels* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §3 canary-telemetry routing, §11 CS §10 note]
- [ ] **AC3** (non-conflation) — Given a canary trip (operational health, not a memory-domain state change), When routed, Then it is NEVER published as a `memory.*` CloudEvent on `studio-spine`. *assert: zero CloudEvents emitted for a canary trip* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §3, §8 Shape 3 Note, CS §10 non-conflation]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given the emitted metric/log, When a canary trips, Then a paging alert fires with a DISTINCT alert per (provider + selectorPackVersion + failureClass) and routes to the extension team / on-call rotation (the FR-BC7 remediation owner). *assert: one alert per (provider,version,class); routed to extension on-call* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §6 failure-taxonomy, §3, FR-D7]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given the four failure classes {NOTFOUND, NOTWRITABLE, AMBIGUOUS, VERIFY}, When each ingests, Then each is alerted separately (a single-candidate miss among several does NOT alert; only zero-candidate does — the client already gates this, api records every trip). *assert: per-class alerting, no class collapse* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §6, §5 selector strategy]
- [ ] **AC6** — Given AD-EXT-5 (client makes zero non-Orvex egress), When telemetry ships, Then it rides ONLY this api HTTP seam — never a direct OTLP/Loki push or broker write from the bundle. *assert: ingest is the only path; api is the sole re-emitter* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §3, §11 cell-lint note]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: tests for schema-valid ingest (AC1), metric+log re-emit (AC2), no-CloudEvent (AC3), per-(provider,version,class) alert routing (AC4/5), seam-only path (AC6).
- [ ] GREEN: `POST` canary-telemetry ingest endpoint (Shape 3 generated type); re-emit OTLP `canary_trip_total` metric + structured Loki log line; explicitly NOT a CloudEvent.
- [ ] Alerting: author the alert RULE (per provider+selectorPackVersion+failureClass) over the emitted metric/log and route it to the extension on-call rotation — the party paged by FR-BC7 remediation. Flag the alert-routing/on-call target as a pack-review confirm (console `L1NVnmRgpy` alert-state surface DISPLAYS but does not DEFINE this rule).
- [ ] Codegen Shape 3 from `orvex-studio-contracts`; drift gate + no `any` across the seam.

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the extension canon sweep (`ViWUZj1MrW`, Architecture: Orvex Studio Extension — Cross-AI Delivery — status DRAFT, pending human doc-ratify per CS §12; reviewer-gate PASSed (opus-adversarial-review), all six extension-pack pages drafted and awaiting PO doc-ratify, none yet canonical).

Server counterparty of the extension canary CLIENT stories ENG-2725 (`[extension]` selector fixtures + breakage heartbeat) and ENG-2726 (`[extension]` fail-loud + team alert) — those DETECT and POST; NO api story implements the INGEST + re-emit + alert-rule side. Verified NOT owned: api's own `E1-S6 Persistence topology + observability + audit stream` emits api's OWN OTel SLIs, not an extension-canary ingest endpoint; the console alert-state surface (`L1NVnmRgpy`) only renders existing alerts. The architecture (§3, §13 F1 review-close) explicitly names `orvex-studio-api` as the SOLE ingesting service — "there is no separate telemetry service to invent."

## 🧪 Testing

`TestCanaryTelemetryIngestEmitsMetricLogAndAlerts` (integration). CS §5: exercise the ingest + re-emit REAL; the OTLP/Loki sinks and the client canary are boundaries. Unit: Shape 3 validator, per-class label mapping.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§0/3.7/5/9/10/11/12 (contract-shape law, design-it-twice, mock-only-true-boundaries, honesty). SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: Security (Zero-Trust, mint-boundary enforcement, personal↔employer firewall), Reliability (fail-loud/fail-safe), Operational Excellence (single authoritative audit). CS §10 telemetry-path non-conflation (metrics/OTLP + logs/Loki only for operational health; CloudEvents only for memory-domain state). cell-lint (metric/log cell-stamped).

## 🔗 References

Architecture `ViWUZj1MrW` §3 (canary-telemetry routing), §6 (failure taxonomy), §8 Shape 3, §11 CS §10 note, §13 F1. Console architecture `L1NVnmRgpy`.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2690** (Shape 3 contract TAG, AC5 dispatch gate), Wave-1-gated on **ENG-2037** / **ENG-2091**.
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2098** (api pack certification).
- [ ] Consumed by extension clients: **ENG-2725** (canary heartbeat), **ENG-2726** (fail-loud + team alert).

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.

gaps.verdict_note: CONFIRMED — verified against live wiki source (ViWUZj1MrW §8 Shape 3, §3 canary-telemetry routing, §6 failure taxonomy) word-for-word; grepped breakdown for 'canary'/'Shape 3' — only extension-side detection stories (ENG-2725/2726) and api's own E1-S6 (its OWN OTel SLIs, no ingest endpoint) exist; console L1NVnmRgpy confirmed display-only, no alert-rule authoring story anywhere. Fixed: same false CANONICAL/ratified claim about ViWUZj1MrW reworded to accurate DRAFT/pending-doc-ratify status.

gaps.requirement: Shape 2 mint tightening — identity mints the delivery token with composedTextRef bound into scope {memory, target, user, composedTextRef} and RE-RESOLVES the personal-vs-employer scope SERVER-SIDE from the live session, failing closed when the client's asserted-scope mismatches (AD-EXT-3, AD-EXT-6, §8 Shape 2, §14 Findings 2 & 6).
gaps.quote: the minted token scope becomes {memory, target, user, composedTextRef} ... identity RE-RESOLVES the personal-vs-employer scope SERVER-SIDE from the live Orvex session at mint time and is AUTHORITATIVE; any client-supplied scope (Shape 2's asserted-scope) is a non-authoritative ASSERTION, used ONLY to detect a client/server mismatch — FAIL CLOSED ... if asserted-scope != server-resolved scope.
gaps.why_uncovered: ENG-2448 owns the BASE AD-8 delivery-token mint but its ACs pin scope == {memory,target,user} with no composedTextRef binding and no asserted-scope server re-resolution — the two load-bearing tightenings the reviewer-gate-passed (draft, pending doc-ratify) canon added post-ENG-2448 (Findings 2 & 6): the anti-content-swap binding and the firewall-subject authority. These specific obligations are unowned; may be folded into ENG-2448 by the ENG-2101 pack.

proposed_issue.title: [identity] Shape 2 mint tightening — bind composedTextRef into the delivery-token scope + server-side asserted-scope re-resolution with fail-closed-on-mismatch (extends ENG-2448)
proposed_issue.body_md:
## 🎯 Story

As **`orvex-studio-identity`** (the mint-boundary enforcement authority, AD-8), I want the delivery-token mint TIGHTENED to the reviewer-gate-passed (draft, pending doc-ratify) Shape 2 — the minted token scope becomes `{memory, target, user, composedTextRef}` (binding the content-hash of the exact FR-CF1-previewed bundle), and the mint RE-RESOLVES the personal-vs-employer scope SERVER-SIDE from the live Orvex session, treating the client's `asserted-scope` as a NON-authoritative assertion that FAILS CLOSED on mismatch, so that no untrusted client can swap composed content after minting and no client-asserted scope can decide the firewall subject.

**Definition of Done:** `TestMintBindsComposedTextRefAndReResolvesScope` (integration) — a mint request `{memoryRef, target, composedTextRef, asserted-scope}` yields a single-target/single-use/short-TTL token whose scope is `{memory, target, user, composedTextRef}` with `user`/scope SERVER-RESOLVED from the live session; when `asserted-scope != server-resolved scope` the mint FAILS CLOSED (no token); a `composedTextRef` change forces a fresh mint (cannot satisfy an already-minted token).
*Dispatch-blocked until the Shape 2 contract TAG exists (ENG-2690 AC5, Wave-1-gated on ENG-2037/ENG-2091) and the identity pack certifies (ENG-2101). May be folded into ENG-2448 by the pack owner.*

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **AC1** — Given a mint request carrying the consented `composedTextRef` (content-hash of the FR-CF1-previewed bundle), When minted, Then the token scope is `{memory, target, user, composedTextRef}` (extends ENG-2448's `{memory,target,user}`). *assert: token scope includes composedTextRef* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 AD-EXT-3, §8 Shape 2]
- [ ] **AC2** — Given the mint, When it resolves scope, Then identity RE-RESOLVES the personal-vs-employer scope SERVER-SIDE from the live Orvex session and mints against THAT — never the client's value. *assert: minted scope == server-resolved, not asserted* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 AD-EXT-6 scope-authority]
- [ ] **AC3** (fail-closed) — Given `asserted-scope != server-resolved scope`, When minting, Then FAIL CLOSED — no token minted, a re-auth/copy-paste signal returned. *assert: mismatch → no token* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 AD-EXT-6, §8 Shape 2 Note]
- [ ] **AC4** — Given an already-minted token, When a fresh precompute CHANGES the composed text (new `composedTextRef`), Then a fresh FR-CF1 consent + fresh mint is REQUIRED — the new text can never silently satisfy the prior token. *assert: composedTextRef change → re-mint required* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 AD-EXT-3, §8 Shape 2 Note]
- [ ] **AC5** — Given an expired/absent live session at mint time, When minting, Then FAIL CLOSED (no unscoped/last-known-scope token). *assert: no-session → no mint* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 AD-EXT-6]
- [ ] **AC6** — Given the mint record, When written, Then the consent + `composedTextRef` binding is persisted for the api Shape 1a hash-equality check to validate against (api never re-derives the text). *assert: mint persists composedTextRef for cross-service hash check* [Source: `ViWUZj1MrW` §8 Shape 1a/Shape 2, §2 AD-EXT-3]

## 🔨 Tasks

- [ ] RED: tests for composedTextRef-in-scope (AC1), server-side scope re-resolution (AC2), asserted!=resolved fail-closed (AC3), composedTextRef-change re-mint (AC4), no-session fail-closed (AC5), persisted binding (AC6).
- [ ] GREEN: extend the ENG-2448 delivery-token mint to the reviewer-gate-passed (draft, pending doc-ratify) Shape 2 — add `composedTextRef` to the minted scope; re-resolve personal-vs-employer scope from the live session; treat `asserted-scope` as a mismatch detector only; persist the consent + composedTextRef binding.
- [ ] Store: extend the consent/mint record schema with `composedTextRef` + the server-resolved scope (cell-stamped).

## 🧠 Context

**🧾 Gap provenance (2026-07-15):** filed from the extension canon sweep (`ViWUZj1MrW`, Architecture: Orvex Studio Extension — Cross-AI Delivery — status DRAFT, pending human doc-ratify per CS §12; reviewer-gate PASSed (opus-adversarial-review), all six extension-pack pages drafted and awaiting PO doc-ratify, none yet canonical).

Adjacent owner **ENG-2448** (`[identity]` Personal↔employer firewall + per-use consent + AD-8 mint-boundary delivery token) owns the BASE mint but its ACs pin scope == `{memory,target,user}` with NO `composedTextRef` binding and NO `asserted-scope` server re-resolution — the two load-bearing tightenings the reviewer-gate-passed (draft, pending doc-ratify) canon added (Findings 2 & 6, §14): the anti-content-swap binding and the firewall-subject authority. This story closes that delta; the identity pack (ENG-2101) may fold it into ENG-2448 rather than stand up a sibling — flag at pack review. Cross-service: api reserves/spends the token and validates `composedTextRef` by hash equality (the api Shape 1a gap); the token flows identity(mint)→api(reserve/spend).

## 🧪 Testing

`TestMintBindsComposedTextRefAndReResolvesScope` (integration). CS §5: exercise the mint + scope re-resolution + consent-record store REAL; the client compose + api validate are cross-service boundaries stubbed at their edges.

## 📏 Guidance

CS `6aMAzsYeQb` §§0/3.7/5/9/10/11/12 (contract-shape law, design-it-twice, mock-only-true-boundaries, honesty). SE-Arch `8sYi523i4t`: Security (Zero-Trust, mint-boundary enforcement, personal↔employer firewall), Reliability (fail-loud/fail-safe), Operational Excellence (single authoritative audit). cell-lint (consent/mint records cell-stamped; `internal/store/postgres/` confinement).

## 🔗 References

Architecture `ViWUZj1MrW` §2 (AD-EXT-3/6), §4 sequence, §8 Shape 2, §14 Findings 2 & 6. identity PRD `cnhla0qRRF` (FR-S5/S6, AD-8); identity Architecture `dQUjrSXhdp`.

## 🔗 Dependencies

- [ ] Blocked by / extends: **ENG-2448** (base delivery-token mint).
- [ ] Blocked by: **ENG-2101** (identity Memory-security pack certification / contract tag), Wave-1-gated on **ENG-2037** / **ENG-2091**; **ENG-2690** (Shape 2 contract shape).
- [ ] Cross-service: the api Shape 1a validate/reserve/spend gap consumes this mint. Consumed by extension clients **ENG-2721** (consent gate), **ENG-2722** (firewall) which PRESENT the token.

## 📡 Protocol

CLAIM → PLAN → PROGRESS → COMMITS ("Part of ENG-NNN", never closes) → HANDOFF → REVIEW (reviewer ≠ implementer) → TICK → DONE (orchestrator-only) → ESCALATE.
